zh.lifeSummary: 當John Hill於1529年出生在英國薩默塞特豪德斯同時，他的父親Sir Robert Hill26歲，母親Margaret Fauntleroy24歲。他和Jane Rodney至少有3個兒子。他去世於1611年在英國瓦維其可爾享年82歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭瓦維其可爾史達科特 奧 阿沃。

nameMeanings.name: Hill
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: topographic name for someone who lived on or by a hill, from Middle English hill, hell, hull ‘hill’ (Old English hyll). Compare Hiller . This surname is also very common among African Americans.
English: possibly in some cases from the personal name Hille, a pet form of some name such as Hilger or Hillary .
German: from a short form of Hildebrand or any of a variety of other names, male and female, containing ancient Germanic hild ‘battle’ as the first element.

variants.name: Hillock
meanings: English and Scottish: topographic name for someone who lived ‘(by the) small hill’, from Middle English hillok, a diminutive of hille (Old English hyll ‘hill’). See Hill .

variants.name: Hills
meanings: English: variant of Hill with genitival -s if a relationship name, attested in Matilda Hilles, 1327 (Somerset), where the genitival form may signify widowhood; or with plural -s if habitational, attested in Johanne de Hyles, 1379 (Bickerstaffe, Lancashire), and Ricardo del Hyles, 1379 (Great Eccleston, Lancashire); or, with post-medieval excrescent -s, probably the most common form.

variants.name: Hiller
meanings: German: metronymic from Hille, a pet form of the female personal name Hildegund (see Hilke ).
German: from a masculine personal beginning with the ancient Germanic element hild ‘strife, battle’.

variants.name: Hil
meanings: Dutch (Van den Hil): topographic name from hil, hul ‘hill’, particularly in the sense of a flight hill above sea level in the southwestern Netherlands.

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: As a boy's name this represents a revival of Middle English Jan, a by‐form of John , or an adoption of the common European form with this spelling. As a girl's name it is a short form of names such as Janet and Janice .

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

en.lifeSummary: When John Hill was born in 1529, in Houndstone, Somerset, England, his father, Sir Robert Hill, was 26 and his mother, Margaret Fauntleroy, was 24. He had at least 3 sons with Jane Rodney. He died in 1611, in Warwickshire, England, at the age of 82, and was buried in Stratford on Avon, Warwickshire, England, United Kingdom.

ja.lifeSummary: John Hillが1529年にイギリスソメルセトホーウンドストーンで生まれた時，父Sir Robert Hillは26歳，母Margaret Fauntleroyは24歳であった。彼はJane Rodneyとの間に少なくとも3人の息子をもうけた。彼は1611年にイギリスワーウィクシュアイアで82歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスワーウィクシュアイアストラトフォード オン アボンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: John Hill(이)가 1529년 영국 서머세트 허운드스톤에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir Robert Hill(은)는 26세였고, 그의 어머니 Margaret Fauntleroy(은)는 24세였다. 그는 Jane Rodney(와)과 적어도 세 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1611년 영국 워윅실르에서 82세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 워윅실르 스트라트퍼드  아폰에 묻혔다.

Он умер в 1611 г. в местности варвиккшир, Англия в возрасте 82 лет и был похоронен в местности стратфорд он авон, варвиккшир, Англия, Великобритания.
